reasoning: Elon Musk is deeply entrenched at Tesla, acting effectively as a founder-CEO with strong board and shareholder loyalty (recently demonstrated by the shareholder vote to reinstate his $56B pay package). Voluntary departure is unlikely because he is currently driving Tesla's major pivot into AI and robotics (Robotaxi, Optimus), which he considers critical to his long-term goals. While he manages multiple companies (SpaceX, xAI, X) and may take on advisory roles in a future US administration, he has historically maintained his CEO title at major ventures rather than stepping entirely away. Involuntary departure due to board action is highly improbable, leaving only tail risks such as sudden regulatory bans (e.g., from the SEC), severe health issues, or unforeseen scandals. The baseline probability for such black-swan events resulting in CEO departure over a single year is roughly 3-5%.
